sections.title: Talking to the golem to start the quest
Talking to the Golem after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Broken clay golem
lines.text: Damage... severe...

lines.speaker: Broken clay golem
lines.text: Damage... severe... Task... incomplete...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Shall I try to repair you?

lines.speaker: Broken clay golem
lines.text: Repairs... needed...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not going to find a conversation here!

lines.speaker: Broken clay golem
lines.text: Graar!

lines.speaker: Broken clay golem
lines.text: Must... not... injure... human...

sections.title: Using the soft clay on the golem
Before starting the quest
After using the first soft clay
After using the second soft clay
After using the third soft clay
After using the fourth soft clay

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Damage repaired...

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Thank you. My body and mind are fully healed.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Now I must complete my task by defeating the great enemy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What enemy?

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: A great demon. It broke through from its dimension to attack the city.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: The golem army was created to fight it. Many were destroyed, but we drove the demon back!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: The demon is still wounded. You must open the portal so that I can strike the final blow and complete my task.

sections.title: Talking to the Golem again after learning about the demon

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: My task is incomplete. You must open the portal so I can defeat the great demon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I open the portal?

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: The four statuettes in the temple must be turned to the correct pattern.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I do not know the pattern. Golems are not permitted to open the portal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes you think you can defeat the demon?

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: If not I, then who else? No living being can destroy the demon. That is why the golems were created in the first place.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: But the demon was badly wounded and elder-demons heal very slowly indeed. It was almost dead when it retreated to its own dimension.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Now that I am repaired, I will be able to destroy it easily!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it.

sections.title: Visiting the Digsite
Talking to Elissa in the Digsite
Talking to Elissa again after learning where the notes are

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you say the notes were?

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: They're on a bookcase in the Exam Centre.

sections.title: Searching the bookcase
Talking to Elissa after reading Varmen's notes

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the statuette that Varmen took back from Uzer?

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: The statuette? Oh, yes...

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: That statuette was the only thing we had to show from that expedition.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: It was very worn, but you can still make out a lot of detail. The Uzerians were expert sculptors. It's a pity we only have that small example.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Now it's on display in the Varrock museum.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: I'm helping with the dig. I'm an expert on Third Age architecture.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: In the Third Age, this was a great city. Look at these giant walls! They put Varrock to shame!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know, Varrock palace is impressive.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Hmph. I don't think it will look this good when it's been buried in the ground for three thousand years!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to the city?

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: No one knows for sure.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: But the Third Age was a time of destruction, when the gods were violently at war.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Many great civilizations were destroyed then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a letter in the desert with your name on.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Ah, so you've found the ruins of Uzer.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: AI wrote that letter to my late husband when he was exploring there.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: that was a great city as well, but the museum could only fund one excavation and this one was closer to home.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: If you're interested in his expedition, the notes he made are in the library in the Exam Centre.

sections.title: Visiting the Varrock Museum
Talking to Curator Haig in the Varrock Museum

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a statuette recovered from the city of Uzer.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Ah yes, a very impressive artefact. The people of that city were excellent sculptors.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: It's in the display case upstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I need to take it away with me.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: What do you want it for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to open a portal to the lair of an elder-demon.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Good heavens! I'd never let you do such a dangerous thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I, er, just want it.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Well, you can't have it! This museum never lets go of its treasures.

sections.title: Pickpocketing Curator Haig
Opening the display case
Talking to Curator Haig after losing the statuette

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a statuette recovered from the city of Uzer.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Ah yes, a very impressive artefact. The people of that city were excellent sculptors.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: It's in the display case upstairs.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: That statuette was stolen recently, but now it's been returned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I need to take it away with me.

sections.title: Returning to Uzer
Using the strange implement on the Golem without the new instructions
Using statuette on alcove
Unlocking the door with statuettes
Trying to turn statuettes after opening the door
Talking to the golem after opening the portal

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: My task is incomplete. You must open the portal so I can defeat the great demon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I opened the portal.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Golems cannot pass through the portal. But the demon will soon emerge. I must ready myself for combat!

sections.title: Entering the portal
Using hammer on throne
Talking to the golem after entering the throne of the demon

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: My task is incomplete. You must open the portal so I can defeat the great demon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay, the demon is dead!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: The demon must be defeated...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, you don't understand. I saw the demon's skeleton. It must have died of its wounds.

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Demon must be defeated! Task incomplete.

sections.title: Talking to the golem again after being told the demon must be defeated

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: My task is incomplete. You must open the portal so I can defeat the great demon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already told you, he's dead!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Task incomplete.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, how am I going to convince you?

sections.title: Taking the phoenix feather

lines.speaker: Desert Phoenix
lines.text: Squawk!

sections.title: Picking a black mushroom
Crushing the mushroom with a pestle and mortar
Crushing the mushroom without having an empty vial
Using the phoenix feather on papyrus
Using a phoenix feather on the black dye
Using the phoenix quill on the papyrus
Using program on golem before using the strange implement
After using the strange implement on the golem in Uzer and adding the new instructions

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: New instructions... Updating program...

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Task complete!

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Thank you. Now my mind is at rest.

sections.title: Post quest
Talking to the Golem

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: Thank you for helping me. A golem can have no greater satisfaction than knowing that its task is complete.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But the whole city is destroyed! Doesn't that bother you?

lines.speaker: Clay golem
lines.text: I was never programmed to appreciate the city. My only purpose was the destruction of the demon, and that is achieved!
